services.title: Power Distribution & Generation
services.metaTitle: Marine MSB/ESB & Generator Service | Levent Marine
services.metaDescription: MSB/ESB faults, AVR hunting, shaft generator commissioning, transformer and shore power surveys. 24h onboard attendance worldwide from Pendik and Wyoming desks.
services.intro: Main and emergency switchboards lose discrimination, AVRs hunt at light load, and shaft generators trip on reverse power without warning. We rebuild the discrimination tree, retune the AVR loop, and clear the synchronising fault before your next port call.
symptoms: AVR hunting +/- 30V at light load on a Stamford HCI544 or similar
Reverse power trip on shaft generator during PTI/PTO transitions
Synchroscope drift, breaker fails to close on dead bus condition
Bus-tie ACB trips on overcurrent during heavy consumer start
Shore power connection trips immediately at cold ironing berth
Emergency generator fails to auto-start within 45s (SOLAS II-1/43)
Transformer hum and oil leak on 440/230V step-down unit
Insulation alarm on 440V bus, IR drops below 0.5 MOhm
rootCauses: Aged Terasaki AR/AT ACB closing coil or undervoltage release out of tolerance
AVR PCB capacitor drift, sensing CT polarity reversed after overhaul
Loose busbar bolt torque, partial discharge on insulator stand-offs
Governor actuator hysteresis combined with mismatched droop settings
Loss of phase reference on sync panel, faulty check-sync (25) relay
whatWeCheck: ACB primary injection and trip unit test on Terasaki/Mitsubishi/ABB Emax
AVR build-up curve, droop CT phasing, parallel kVAR sharing under load
Generator stator IR and PI with Megger MIT525 at 1000V DC
Differential (87G) stability and REF relay timing on neutral CT
Sync, check-sync (25), reverse power (32R), loss of field (40) settings
Feeder and tie breaker discrimination per IEC 60909 short-circuit study
Transformer winding ratio, vector group, no-load and load loss
Shore power interlock and ground fault monitoring per IEC/IEEE 80005-1
tools: OMICRON CMC 356 secondary injection set
SVERKER 900 primary injection up to 3000A
Megger MIT525 5kV insulation tester with PI/DAR
Fluke 1587 FC insulation multimeter
FLIR T540 thermal camera for busbar thermography
Hioki PW3198 power quality analyser
Yokogawa CW240 clamp-on power meter

exampleCase.headline: AVR hunting cleared in 6 hours, vessel sailed on schedule
exampleCase.summary: Master reported 420-465V swing on No.2 D/G during cargo gear test. Onboard within 4 hours from Pendik. Found AVR sensing CT loose at terminal X7, droop CT phase reversed after last yard period. Reterminated, rephased, retuned PID, load tested 0/50/100/110 percent. Class-acceptable record delivered same evening.

relatedSupply: Stamford AVR MX341/MX321 replacement modules
Terasaki AR208S/AR316S ACB trip units
Schneider Masterpact NW closing/opening coils
Woodward 2301D and DPG governor controllers
DEIF AGC-4 and Selco T2000 paralleling relays

services.title: Propulsion, Thrusters & Deck Machinery
services.metaTitle: Bow Thruster, Crane & Windlass Electrical Service
services.metaDescription: Bow thruster pitch faults, deck crane VFD trips, hatch cover hydraulics, windlass overcurrent. Electrical fault-finding and motor rewind coordination, onboard or remote.
services.intro: Bow thruster pitch sticks at 60 percent, the No.2 deck crane trips on regen during slewing, and the windlass overload relay won't reset after winter lay-up. We trace the actual electrical fault path instead of swapping cards on guesswork.
symptoms: Bow thruster CPP feedback drift, pitch indicator stuck mid-range
Deck crane VFD F0007 overvoltage trip during boom lowering
Hatch cover motor single-phasing after rain ingress to JB
Windlass main contactor chatter, brake coil burnt on second attempt
Cargo pump motor IR drops to 0.3 MOhm after tank cleaning
Air compressor auto-start fails, pressure switch contact pitted
Steering gear running motor temperature alarm at 85 deg C
Provision crane joystick zero-position drift causing slow creep
rootCauses: Slip ring carbon dust contamination in CPP feedback synchro
VFD braking resistor open circuit, regen energy with no dump path
Aged ABB M3BP bearing grease, NDE bearing current pitting
Salt bridge across terminal block inside motor terminal box
Aged Bussmann fuses with degraded I2t, nuisance trips on starting surge
whatWeCheck: Motor IR and PI per IEEE 43, polarisation index 2.0 minimum
VFD parameter set on ABB ACS880, Siemens G150, Yaskawa A1000
Soft starter SCR firing angle and bypass on ABB PSE/PSTX
Bearing condition via shock pulse (SPM) and motor current signature analysis
CPP hydraulic-electric interface, OD box feedback transducer linearity
Crane slew, hoist, luff limit switches and overload load cell calibration
Windlass brake release sequencing, gypsy clutch limit switch wiring
Hatch cover sequence interlocks per yard wiring diagram revision
tools: Megger MIT525 5kV IR tester and Baker AWA-IV surge comparison
SKF MicroVibe vibration meter and shock pulse probe
Fluke 438-II motor analyser with current signature module
FLIR T540 thermography for hot bearing and terminal scans
Druck DPI 620 calibrator for 4-20mA and PT100
Laser shaft alignment kit (SKF TKSA 41)

exampleCase.headline: Bow thruster restored before pilot boarding window
exampleCase.summary: Pilot refused unberthing without working bow thruster. CPP pitch frozen at -15 percent. Boarded with launch in 90 minutes. Found OD box synchro brush stuck, drained moisture, lapped slip rings, re-zeroed feedback. Full thrust both directions confirmed quayside, vessel sailed on next tide. Total downtime under 5 hours.

relatedSupply: ABB ACS880/ACS580 drive spare control boards
Siemens SIMATIC ET200 remote I/O for crane PLC
Stahl ATEX junction boxes and cable glands for tankers
Pilz PNOZ safety relays for hatch cover interlocks
Heidenhain and Lika resolver/encoder spares for CPP feedback

services.title: Navigation, Bridge & GMDSS
services.metaTitle: Marine Radar, ECDIS & GMDSS Service | Levent Marine
services.metaDescription: Furuno, JRC and Sperry bridge service. Radar magnetron, ECDIS faults, gyro alignment, GMDSS annual surveys and PSC defect rectification worldwide, class acceptable.
services.intro: ECDIS freezes during chart loading, the X-band radar shows a 30-degree blind sector, and GMDSS battery capacity is below 80 percent of nameplate. We fix the bridge stack before the surveyor finds it.
symptoms: Furuno FAR-2228 weak echo, magnetron current below 1.5 mA
JRC JAN-9201 ECDIS frozen during ENC update, S-57 import error
Sperry NaviGat 4000 gyro heading offset 3 deg from GPS COG
Autopilot rudder hunting +/- 2 deg on calm sea
AIS Class A intermittent target loss, VHF antenna SWR > 2.5
VDR annual performance test failure on bridge audio channel
GMDSS reserve battery falls below 6 hours discharge capacity
Echo sounder ghost echoes in shallow water under 20m
rootCauses: Magnetron end-of-life after 3000+ hours, cathode emission degraded
ECDIS HDD bad sectors or RAM ECC errors after years of vibration
Gyro support gimbal bearing wear, follow-up motor brush worn
Salt corrosion on AIS antenna N-connector base, shield broken
Sulphated GMDSS lead-acid bank, individual cell below 2.05V
whatWeCheck: Magnetron current, transceiver tune voltage, scanner gearbox noise
ECDIS performance per IMO MSC.232(82), redundancy switchover test
Gyro alignment vs known transit bearing and dual GPS heading reference
Autopilot Mk-7 PID gains and rudder feedback unit linearity
GMDSS annual radio survey per Reg. IV/15, battery discharge test
AIS static/dynamic data verification and VDR 12-hour record extraction
VHF/MF/HF antenna VSWR with Bird 4304A wattmeter and dummy load
NMEA data flow audit between gyro, GPS, log, ECDIS and ARPA
tools: Bird 4304A through-line wattmeter and 50 ohm dummy load
Anritsu MS2720T spectrum analyser
Furuno FSV and JRC service terminal
Megger BITE3 battery impedance tester
Actisense NGT-1 NMEA protocol analyser
Garmin GPS19x reference for gyro cross-check

exampleCase.headline: GMDSS annual survey cleared in single attendance
exampleCase.summary: Owner requested GMDSS annual ahead of voyage to USEC. Found reserve battery cell #6 at 1.92V, MF/HF antenna VSWR 3.1 from corroded base insulator. Replaced cell, cleaned base, retested. Class radio surveyor attended, certificate endorsed same day. No delay to scheduled departure for Norfolk.

relatedSupply: Furuno magnetron MAF1565N and MG5436
JRC ECDIS spare LCD panels and processor boards
Sperry gyro spheres and support unit bearings
GMDSS sealed lead-acid 12V/100Ah batteries
Jotron Tron 60S EPIRB hydrostatic release units

services.title: Automation, AMS, IAS & PMS
services.metaTitle: Marine AMS, PMS & PLC Automation Service
services.metaDescription: AMS nuisance alarms, IAS HMI faults, PMS load sharing, PLC and SCADA troubleshooting on Kongsberg, Praxis, ABB, Selco. IACS cyber notation prep and FAT/SAT.
services.intro: AMS prints 400 nuisance alarms per watch, the PMS will not auto-start the next priority generator on heavy consumer demand, and the engine control PLC has a leaking battery on the CPU. We rebuild the alarm philosophy and the load-dependent logic so the duty engineer can sleep.
symptoms: AMS alarm flood, >10 alarms per 10 minutes (EEMUA 191 breach)
PMS fails to start next generator at 85 percent load threshold
Praxis Megachief HMI black screen after random period of hours
Tank level transmitter (Hawk, KSR Kuebler) reads 0 percent on full tank
Boiler control 4-20mA loop drift, drum level oscillation
Rotork IQ3 actuator fails to report intermediate position
Engine control PLC battery low warning, backup memory at risk
Modbus RTU comms drop between PLC and remote I/O panel
rootCauses: PMS load-share CT phasing error after maintenance, kW sense reversed
HMI graphics card thermal failure, ventilation filter blocked
PT100 transmitter calibration drift beyond +/- 0.5 percent of span
Modbus RS485 termination resistor missing, CRC errors from reflections
Aged lithium backup (BR2/3A) on PLC CPU below 2.7V
whatWeCheck: PMS load-share, deadband, blackout recovery, heavy consumer sequencing
AMS alarm rationalisation against EEMUA 191 and ISA 18.2
PLC scan time, memory utilisation, hot spare CPU sync on redundant racks
All 4-20mA loops with HART communicator (Emerson 475)
Valve travel, deadband and stroke time per signed loop sheet
Cyber baseline per IACS UR E26/E27 and IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3
HMI graphics vs P&ID, alarm setpoints vs ESD matrix
Power supply redundancy and 24VDC bus stability under fault
tools: Emerson 475 HART field communicator
Druck DPI 620 and Fluke 754 process calibrators
B&B USB-COMi-SI RS485 bus analyser
Wireshark with Modbus/Profibus/CAN dissectors
RSLogix 5000, Siemens TIA Portal, CODESYS service licenses
Tek MSO44 mixed-signal scope for fieldbus integrity

exampleCase.headline: PMS blackout recovery logic restored before laden departure
exampleCase.summary: Vessel suffered partial blackout during cargo loading, PMS failed to restart No.3 D/G. Mobilised by air via Perth, found load-sharing line CT phase reversal after recent overhaul. Reterminated, simulated heavy consumer step, verified auto-start within 12 seconds. Class witnessed, vessel sailed laden 11 hours after attendance began.

relatedSupply: Praxis Megachief and Megaguard I/O cards
Kongsberg K-Chief 600 RIO modules and HMI panels
Siemens S7-1500 CPU and ET200SP remote I/O
Allen-Bradley ControlLogix 1756 spare cards
Phoenix Contact and Weidmuller relay interface modules

services.title: Fire, Gas & Safety Systems
services.metaTitle: Marine Fire Detection, CO2 & PA/GA Service
services.metaDescription: Autronica, Consilium fire detection faults, CO2 and HI-FOG release logic, PA/GA intelligibility, gas detection and CCTV. SOLAS Ch. II-2 class survey ready.
services.intro: The Autronica panel shows 14 zone faults from the cargo hold smoke loop, the PA/GA fails the speech transmission index in the engine room, and the CO2 release station has not been function-tested since the last special survey. We bring all of it back to SOLAS Ch. II-2 condition.
symptoms: Autronica BS-100 zone fault, loop current outside 18-22 mA window
Consilium Salwico CS3000 detector false alarms in galley
PA/GA STI below 0.5 in engine room (IEC 60849 / MSC.808)
CO2 release pilot solenoid fails 24VDC continuity test
Gas detector (Riken Keiki, GMI) zero drift on accommodation IR sensor
CCTV (Bosch, Hernis) image black on cargo hold camera 3
Emergency lighting battery duration below 60 min (SOLAS II-1/42)
Fixed water mist (HI-FOG) section valve solenoid 0 mA on test
rootCauses: Loop shield discontinuity after recent welding in cargo hold
Detector head soot loading beyond chamber sensitivity threshold
Loudspeaker line short-to-ground after deck wash water ingress
Pilot CO2 cylinder pressure below 50 bar, micro-switch out of position
IR sensor lens contamination, calibration gas cylinder expired
whatWeCheck: Fire loop end-to-end with Autronica/Consilium service tool
Detector chamber sensitivity per maker procedure and class survey form
PA/GA STI measurement with B&K 2270 in each zone
CO2 release matrix: door close, fan stop, alarm horn, time delay
Gas detector bump and span with traceable cal gas (10% LEL methane)
Emergency lighting full-discharge test, lux at escape routes
CCTV signal level, IR illuminator and NVR retention period
Quick-closing valves and fire dampers, remote release function
tools: Autronica AutroMaster service laptop and loop analyser
Consilium SW3000 service tool and aerosol detector tester
B&K 2270 sound level analyser with STI/STIPA module
Cal gas: CH4 50%LEL, H2S 10ppm, CO 100ppm, O2 19%
Solo 330 functional smoke/heat tester pole
FLIR T540 for PA amplifier cabinet thermal scan

exampleCase.headline: Pre-PSC fire system defects cleared in 36 hours
exampleCase.summary: Owner expected PSC at next US port. Loop 4 (cargo hold smoke) had three faults, PA/GA STI 0.42 in engine room. Replaced two corroded TSP cables, cleaned 18 detector heads, retuned PA crossover and added two loudspeakers in noisy zones. Final STI 0.61, all zones healthy. Vessel passed PSC at Houston with zero deficiencies.

relatedSupply: Autronica BH-310, BJ-310 detector heads and bases
Consilium Salwico cargo and accommodation detectors
Riken Keiki RX-8500 and SP-220 gas detector spares
CO2 release pilot solenoids and pressure switches (Tyco/Ansul)
Bosch Praesideo and DNH PA/GA amplifier cards and loudspeakers

services.title: Lighting & Navigation Lights
services.metaTitle: Navigation Light & LED Retrofit | Levent Marine
services.metaDescription: Navigation light controller faults, LED retrofit with COLREG type approval, cargo hold floodlights, engine room and emergency lighting upgrades, class type-approved sets.
services.intro: The starboard sidelight controller alarms but the lamp is healthy, the cargo hold floods drop two breakers when all four are switched, and the wheelhouse dimmer flickers on the new LED panel lights. We finish the job with full COLREG type-approval paperwork in the file.
symptoms: Nav light failure alarm with bulb intact, current sensor below threshold
Cargo hold floodlight 1000W MH lamp restrike time over 15 minutes
Engine room emergency LED panel flicker on 24V DC ripple
Wheelhouse dimmer buzz on new LED fixtures, no smooth dim curve
Navigation light controller switchover relay welded contacts
External floodlight IP rating compromised after gland refit
Anchor light lamp life under 200 hours, voltage spike on switching
rootCauses: LED retrofit current draw below original incandescent failure threshold
Capacitor degraded on MH ballast, restrike voltage insufficient
Switch-mode driver in cheap retrofit LED incompatible with leading-edge dimmer
Salt water ingress to nav light glass-to-base seal after years on bridge wing
Inrush current of LED bank exceeds breaker B-curve trip threshold
whatWeCheck: Nav light type approval certificate vs vessel COLREG arc/range
Controller current sensing threshold matched to retrofit LED lamp draw
IP rating and corrosion of nav light housing and lens gasket
Emergency lighting lux vs IMO Res. A.752(18) at escape routes
Inrush current of LED panels and floodlight banks with Fluke i400s
Cargo hold floodlight beam angle and uniformity for cargo work safety
Dimmer compatibility (trailing edge, 0-10V, DALI) per fitting datasheet
tools: Konica Minolta T-10A illuminance meter
Fluke 1735 power logger for harmonic analysis
Yokogawa CW240 clamp meter with inrush capture
DNV and DEKRA type approval cross-reference database
Insulation tester for emergency lighting bus integrity

exampleCase.headline: Full LED retrofit on five holds with type approval pack
exampleCase.summary: Owner targeted 40 percent lighting energy reduction and PSC-proof nav light file. Replaced 24 cargo hold floods with 200W LED type-approved units, upgraded 6 navigation lights to dual-LED COLREG-approved with electronic monitor. Energy log showed 47 percent reduction over voyage. Class-stamped type approval file delivered to office.

relatedSupply: Glamox MIR and SVS LED type-approved fittings
Den Haan Rotterdam nav lights and electronic controllers
Phoenix Products PXLM marine LED floodlights
Aqua Signal Series 41/55 type-approved nav light spares
Hella marine LED bridge and accommodation luminaires

services.title: Electrical Testing & Certification
services.metaTitle: ACB Test, Insulation & Protection Relay Certification
services.metaDescription: Primary and secondary injection of ACB/MCCB, motor insulation testing, protection relay calibration with OMICRON. Class-acceptable reports for DNV, ABS, BV, Lloyd's, TL.
services.intro: Class wants a primary-injection certificate on every ACB above 800A, the insulation log shows three motors below 1 MOhm, and the protection relay file is missing the last two pickup curves. We deliver the test pack the surveyor signs.
symptoms: ACB long-time pickup outside +/- 10 percent of nameplate (IEC 60947-2)
Motor stator IR below 1 MOhm or PI below 2.0 (IEEE 43)
Differential relay (87) stability fails under through-fault simulation
Earth fault (51N/64) timing curve mismatched to coordination study
Thermal scan shows 25K rise on bus joint vs reference temperature
Cable continuity fails on spare core after recent yard cabling
HV switchboard partial discharge above 100 pC (IEC 60270)
Surge arrester leakage current trending up year on year
rootCauses: Trip unit aging or firmware out of date on Terasaki/ABB/Mitsubishi ACB
Carbonisation in motor overhang due to repeated load surges
CT saturation due to mismatched ratio vs short-circuit duty
Loose joint torque on copper busbar, 60Nm spec but found 18Nm
Cable insulation degradation in engine room ambient above 55 deg C
whatWeCheck: Primary injection of ACB main contacts with calibrated SVERKER 900
Trip unit timing at 1.5x, 3x, 6x, 10x In on long-time and short-time
Instantaneous element pickup and contact opening time below 50 ms
Generator and motor protection (50/51, 27/59, 81, 32, 40, 87)
Cable continuity, IR and HV pressure test per IEC 60092
Bus bar joint thermography under load (FLIR T540, emissivity 0.85)
Earthing system integrity, hull bond resistance below 0.1 ohm
Test certificates per DNV-CG-0339 and IACS UR M68
tools: OMICRON CMC 356 with control and test universe
SVERKER 900 primary injection 0-3000A continuous
Megger MIT525 5kV with PI and step voltage
Megger MFT1741 multifunction installation tester
FLIR T540 thermal camera 464x348 IR resolution
Doble F6150e for high-end protection relay testing
HV pressure test set AC/DC up to 15kV portable

exampleCase.headline: Special survey electrical test pack delivered in 4 days
exampleCase.summary: Yard required full 5-yearly electrical certification pack. Tested 14 ACBs (Terasaki AR series), 4 generator protection relays (DEIF MPR-3), 38 MCCBs, 62 motor IR/PI records. Two ACBs failed long-time timing, recalibrated. One generator differential CT found shorted, replaced. Class-accepted file submitted, no outstanding electrical conditions on certificate.

relatedSupply: Terasaki, ABB Emax, Schneider Masterpact trip units
DEIF and Selco generator protection relay spares
SIBA, Bussmann marine HRC fuses (gG, aM)
Mersen surge protection devices for 440V and 230V bus
Class-acceptable test report templates and binder packs

services.title: Commissioning & Retrofit Engineering
services.metaTitle: Marine FAT/SAT, VFD & Shore Power Retrofit
services.metaDescription: Newbuild and retrofit electrical commissioning, VFD installation, LED conversion, shore power IEC/IEEE 80005-1, battery hybrid integration and IACS cyber baseline.
services.intro: The yard delivered the VFD package without a load test, the shore power retrofit needs a TT-to-IT scheme study, and the cyber notation requires a baseline before next class. We write the FAT/SAT script, run it, and hand back a signed file the owner can audit.
symptoms: VFD fails FAT load test above 80 percent due to DC bus ripple
Shore power earth fault monitor mis-coordinated with shore RCD
Battery hybrid DC bus precharge fails on cold start
Yard cabling crosstalk on shielded analog signals to engine room
LED retrofit dimmer drops out at 20 percent setting
Cyber notation gap: no asset inventory, no patch baseline, no segmentation
rootCauses: VFD DC bus capacitor reforming missed after long storage
Shore power scheme mismatch ship IT vs shore TN-S without isolation transformer
BMS CAN bus termination missing, cells offline
Yard pulled signal and power cables in common tray without segregation
Cyber notation not scoped in original yard contract scope of supply
whatWeCheck: Full FAT script vs spec, witnessed loop-by-loop with sign-off
VFD harmonic compliance to IEEE 519, ITHD below 5 percent at PCC
Shore power one-line and protection per IEC/IEEE 80005-1
Battery hybrid SOC accuracy, cell balancing, BMS communication
Cyber baseline: asset list, OT/IT segmentation, USB/remote access policy
Software version control and rollback procedure on PLC/HMI
Crew familiarisation training plan and signed attendance record
As-built drawing redline incorporation and final issue to class
tools: Hioki PW3198 power quality analyser, class A
Fluke Norma 5000 power analyser for VFD efficiency
Tenable.ot or Claroty for read-only OT baseline scan
Wireshark with ICS dissectors for network audit
Test packs aligned to IACS UR E22, E26, E27
CMMS export tools (Star IPS, AMOS, Shipnet) for handover

exampleCase.headline: Shore power and battery hybrid commissioning, IEC compliant
exampleCase.summary: Owner contracted us as third-party commissioning agent. Identified shore power neutral grounding mismatch in week 3, redesigned with isolation transformer to satisfy class. Battery hybrid FAT showed BMS comm fault, root-caused to missing CAN termination. Final SAT at sea trial passed all 47 test cases. Vessel signed off for delivery on contractual date.

relatedSupply: ABB ACS880 and Danfoss VLT marine VFD packages
Cavotec MoorMaster and shore power plug-and-socket systems
Corvus and Leclanche marine battery rack components
Nozomi Networks Guardian sensor for OT monitoring
EPLAN and AutoCAD Electrical engineering software

services.title: Emergency Attendance & Remote ETO
services.metaDescription: 24h emergency onboard attendance, retainer remote ETO desk, PSC Green Pass support, P&I and class incident assistance. Pendik and Wyoming dual-shift coverage.
services.intro: The duty engineer calls at 0240 local with a blackout in progress, the master needs a remote ETO opinion before signing a PSC observation, and the P&I surveyor wants an independent electrical expert at the gangway by sunrise. We answer the phone and we attend.
symptoms: Total or partial blackout, emergency generator running, ETO ashore
PSC officer raises electrical observation, master needs second opinion
P&I survey scheduled within 12 hours after cargo incident
Equipment failure during voyage, manufacturer support outside hours
Class condition issued, owner needs rectification plan within 24 hours
Owner shore office needs independent technical advice on yard invoice
rootCauses: Single point of failure on automation, control transformer or 24V supply
Drawing inconsistency between as-built and revised wiring after retrofit
Lack of pre-departure functional test on critical safety systems
Crew change with insufficient handover on local panel modifications
whatWeCheck: Initial remote triage by phone, photo, schematic and live screen-share
Mobilisation lead time to Turkish ports (Pendik base) within 4 hours
Worldwide mobilisation via Wyoming desk for Americas and Asia time zones
On arrival: blackout root cause, restart procedure, documented chronology
PSC observation wording analysed against class and flag interpretation
P&I-grade independent technical report with photo and instrument log
Handover note and follow-up action list for permanent fix
tools: Mobile kit: insulation tester, scope, thermal camera, calibrator
Iridium and Inmarsat data plans for offshore troubleshooting
Secure remote access via TeamViewer/AnyDesk with vessel firewall
Incident report template aligned with IMO MSC.1/Circ.1432
Local agent network for visa, launch and customs clearance

exampleCase.headline: Blackout at 0310, vessel on own power by 0540
exampleCase.summary: Master called retainer hotline at 0310 with no main bus and emergency generator on. Remote ETO walked duty engineer through manual restart and identified failed AVR sensing fuse remotely. Local team boarded by launch at 0440 with spare AVR module. Bus restored 0540, full root cause report to owner office by 0900 same morning.

relatedSupply: Emergency kit: AVR, governor module, sync check relay, fuses
Common Terasaki and Schneider ACB trip coils in Pendik
PT100 transmitters, 4-20mA isolators, 24V power supplies
Annual retainer plans with priority response SLA
Owner-customised critical spare list maintained on shore

services.title: Class Survey & PSC Preparation
services.metaTitle: Class Intermediate, Special Survey & PSC Prep
services.metaDescription: Intermediate, Annual, Special survey electrical prep, PSC mock inspection, CII rating support, SOLAS compliance. DNV, BV, ABS, Lloyd's, TL approved reports.
services.intro: The Intermediate is in nine weeks, the last PSC came back with three electrical observations the office never closed, and the CII rating is sliding towards D. We do the mock inspection, write the rectification plan, and close every item on the file.
symptoms: Outstanding class condition on electrical, deadline approaching
Recurring PSC observations on emergency lighting or nav light alarms
CII rating dropping due to auxiliary load profile inefficiency
Owner not ready for Special Survey scope sent by class
SOLAS Ch. II-1 electrical drawings out of date vs as-built
Missing test records for items inspected at last class attendance
rootCauses: No systematic pre-survey electrical inspection in vessel SMS
Crew turnover loses institutional knowledge of past defects
Office and superintendent not aligned on rectification scope
Lack of an electrical KPI dashboard for fleet-wide trends
whatWeCheck: Mock class survey covering electrical scope of intermediate/special
Test record gap analysis vs last survey close-out report
Crew drills and familiarisation logs per STCW and ISM
Class drawing register and as-built consistency check
PSC concentrated inspection campaign topic review for current year
CII calculation review per IMO MEPC.354(78), aux engine optimisation
Owner office briefing pack and superintendent walk-through
Final defect list with priority, cost estimate and lead time
tools: DNV-CG-0339, ABS Steel Vessel Rules, BV NR467 reference set
Paris and Tokyo MoU PSC observation database last 24 months
IACS UR E series interpretation library
CII calculator aligned to IMO MEPC.354(78)
GoPro Hero 12 evidence kit with anti-static enclosure

exampleCase.headline: Special Survey passed with zero electrical conditions
exampleCase.summary: Owner requested pre-class attendance 6 weeks before Special Survey window. Mock survey identified 38 items (14 critical), all closed by week 5. Class surveyor (Lloyd's Register) attended, signed without electrical condition. PSC at first US port post-survey resulted in zero observations. Owner extended retainer to sister vessels.

relatedSupply: Class-acceptable test report templates and binder system
Crew familiarisation video pack in TR/EN/RU/PH
Photo evidence package for closed defects (before/after)
PSC checklist customised to flag and recent MoU campaigns
Annual retainer with quarterly fleet-wide compliance reviews

services.title: Insulation Diagnostics & Hidden Earth Faults
services.metaTitle: Marine IR Diagnostics & Hidden Earth Fault Finding
services.metaDescription: Low insulation troubleshooting, hidden earth faults on 440V bus, intermittent trips, motor and cable IR/PI testing. Root cause analysis with class-acceptable report.
services.intro: The earth lamp test shows 0.4 MOhm on the 440V bus and nobody can find it, the bow thruster motor megs 800 kOhm cold but 4 MOhm warm, and the generator trips on earth fault every third start. We map the network, isolate the path, and write the report.
symptoms: Insulation alarm on 440V bus, IR fluctuates 0.2 to 0.8 MOhm
Intermittent earth fault on engine room lighting, weather dependent
Motor IR drops below 1 MOhm only after several hours of running
Generator earth fault relay trips on third or fourth start of the day
PI below 1.5 on cargo pump motor after tank cleaning operation
Cable IR fails after recent yard hot work in adjacent space
Heater element earth leak in galley equipment, RCD nuisance trip
rootCauses: Moisture absorption in motor stator winding overhang insulation
Cable insulation damaged by hot work, fault appears under load expansion
Salt deposits in JB creating low-resistance bridge when humid
Aged heater element with cracked sheath, fault only when hot
Multiple parallel small leakages summing to single alarm threshold
whatWeCheck: Network segmentation: split bus and test each branch separately
Motor IR cold vs hot, polarisation index per IEEE 43
Step voltage test on cables to identify weak insulation level
Cable thermography under load to find local hot spots
Capacitor and EMC filter contributions to apparent earth leakage
Time-domain reflectometer (TDR) on suspect cable runs
Targeted vs systematic approach based on insulation history log
Drying of motor winding with controlled current per IEEE 95
tools: Megger MIT525 5kV with step voltage and DAR/PI
Baker AWA-IV winding analyser with surge comparison
Megger CFL535G time-domain reflectometer
FLIR T540 thermal imager for hot-spot diagnostics under load
Fluke 1587 FC for targeted IR measurements
Sonel MIC-10k1 10kV tester for HV bus and large machines
Bender IRDH575 portable insulation monitor for live networks

exampleCase.headline: Hidden earth fault traced in 8 hours after 3 days of crew search
exampleCase.summary: Crew searched for an earth fault for 72 hours, lost in capacitor and EMC filter network of 14 VFDs. Boarded with Bender portable monitor, isolated bus by feeder, found fault inside one VFD output filter capacitor leaking to chassis. Replaced filter capacitor, IR restored to 50 MOhm. Full diagnostic report with thermography evidence submitted to owner and class.

relatedSupply: Bender IRDH575 and ISOMETER modules for ship IT networks
VFD output filter capacitors (Schaffner, EPCOS)
Heat-shrink insulation kits for motor terminal lug repair
Cable repair kits IEC 60092 compliant (3M Scotchcast)
Moisture absorber heaters for stored spare motors
